title: Around the Imperial Palace
excerpt: The moat with the city stacked along one bank, the clipped black pines of the outer garden, then Nijubashi. A loop where the towers never quite leave the frame.

title: From the KITTE roof
excerpt: Tokyo Station's red brick seen from above, platforms fanning out behind it, glass towers all around. The Marunouchi rooftop everyone climbs up for, and the reason why.

title: Tokyo International Forum
excerpt: Up out of the underground passages and into Viñoly's glass hall, where the ceiling lifts away into a lattice of white ribs. The way in mattered as much as the room.

title: Garden pond, morning
excerpt: Clipped shrubs, a maple turning, the still water doing the rest. A small Tokyo garden first thing, with the city standing in close behind the hedge.

title: At the counter
excerpt: A single piece of silver-skinned nigiri on a bamboo leaf, ginger and scallion on top, gari beside it. The sign behind promises Ōma tuna; this came first.

title: An atrium cat and a quiet staircase
excerpt: A cat in a spacesuit piloting a rocket over a shopping atrium, then a travertine staircase quiet as a chapel. Two interiors a minute apart, opposite moods.

title: Ginza to Marunouchi, Sunday afternoon
excerpt: A man in a Louis Vuitton kimono owning a Ginza crossing, then a red postbox and a building wearing its garden, a few blocks over in Marunouchi. Three frames from one walk.

title: Skytree from the bridge
excerpt: The tower is nominally the subject, but the sky took up most of the frame, and most of the work.

title: Chairs as portraits
excerpt: Two chairs lit from behind in their cases at Designmuseum Danmark, photographed straight on like portraits: an Eames LCW in molded plywood, and Arne Jacobsen's blue Lily with its curling arms.

title: Morning at the Glyptotek
excerpt: Two from the Glyptotek on a quiet morning: Kai Nielsen's marble water mother in the planted Winter Garden, and a visitor who happened to match the rose walls of the painting rooms.

title: Copenhagen, late evening
excerpt: Two from the same golden hour: a window where the harbor and a wall of portraits overlap in reflection, and Nyhavn with the light running along the old fronts.

title: Copenhagen harbor, evening
excerpt: Three from the inner harbor on a late-June evening: new housing on the Christianshavn waterfront, the Opera House across the water, and the fountain in front of it where two children were deciding how close to get. The light this far north holds long past dinner.

title: Copenhagen, behind the street
excerpt: Three spaces a few minutes apart: a courtyard reached through a dark gateway, the spiral ramp of the Round Tower, the glass dome at the Glyptotek. The city does a lot with the light it lets in slowly.
